boundary_rule_compliance.rule: e2e specs publish through the app, never via raw WebSocket to strfry (CLAUDE.md E2E tests section)
boundary_rule_compliance.compliance: groups-abandon-last-member.spec.ts contains no WebSocket usage anywhere. The single KeyPackage publish the scenario needs happens as a side effect of the real create-group flow (create-group-btn -> create-group-name-input -> create-group-submit-btn), driven entirely through page interactions on the real app UI. No event is hand-signed or sent by the test process.

boundary_rule_compliance.rule: AC-FLOW-3 — spec file naming routes the test into the relay bucket
boundary_rule_compliance.compliance: File is app/tests/e2e/groups-abandon-last-member.spec.ts, matching playwright.config.ts:36-41's testMatch: ['groups-*.spec.ts', 'dm-*.spec.ts'] glob (gated by E2E_GROUPS). Confirmed via `E2E_GROUPS=1 npx playwright test --list tests/e2e/groups-abandon-last-member.spec.ts`, which lists all 3 tests under the chromium/relay project.

boundary_rule_compliance.rule: AC-STRUCT-2 — no test-only bypass of the real component tree
boundary_rule_compliance.compliance: The spec drives the actual LeaveGroupButton component via testid clicks only (create-group-btn, leave-group-btn, abandon-group-confirm-btn) — no direct import of leaveGroupImpl, leaveEligibility, or MarmotContext internals from the test process, and no mocked network/relay layer. The abandon path is reached exclusively through real user-visible interactions.

solo_group_creation.contract: Lift the create-only block (no invite step) from groups-lifecycle.spec.ts:47-59 / groups-leave-intent.spec.ts:99-109, per exploration.json's solo_group_gap note — no existing helper covers 'create a group, invite nobody.'
solo_group_creation.notes: Inlined directly in the spec (create-group-btn -> create-group-modal-content visible -> fill create-group-name-input -> create-group-submit-btn -> group text visible -> modal not visible -> dismissErrorOverlay -> 3s settle). Not extracted to a shared helper — this is the only spec that needs it, and the story brief's scope note treats a new shared helper as optional, not required. No existing helper's behavior was modified.

abandon_modal_assertion.contract: Assert abandon-group-notice and abandon-group-confirm-btn visible on a solo group's leave modal; assert last-admin-blocked-notice is NOT visible (pins AC-SELECT-1's admin-first-would-fail ordering at the e2e layer).

url_arrival_assertion.notes: See baseline.json#discriminating_evidence.AC-FLOW-1_url_anchor_gotcha — implemented via expect.poll on pathname+search rather than toHaveURL(regex), since the latter matches against the full absolute URL and can never satisfy a literal ^/ anchor. The anchoring intent (reject '/groups/?id=...' as a false positive) is preserved exactly.

reload_purge_check.contract: AC-FLOW-2 — reload after abandon must still show the group absent.
reload_purge_check.notes: page.reload() followed by the same readiness-gated wait and a fresh getByText(GROUP_NAME) absence assertion, structurally separate from the pre-reload check in the same test.
